title: Robust Spicy Ethiopian Lentils Injera
description: A spicy and springy Ethiopian dish featuring flaxseed and lentils, perfect for meal prep. This halal-friendly recipe is pressure-cooked to perfection and served with injera.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
In a blender, mix the flaxseed with 6 tablespoons of water to create a flaxseed gel.
Heat oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat and saute the onion, garlic, and ginger until the onion is translucent.
Add the berbere spice, tomato paste, and flaxseed gel to the pressure cooker and stir for 1 minute.
Add the lentils, vegetable broth, and salt to the pressure cooker and stir to combine.
Close the lid and pressure-cook for 20 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes.
Serve the lentils with injera and your choice of toppings.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
